question: An ad-hoc sub-process is handled by a job worker. The worker tries to complete its current job with an `adHocSubProcess` job result that both activates new inner elements and signals that the `completionCondition` is fulfilled. What does the docs say about this?

options.text: Zeebe activates the new elements first, then completes the ad-hoc sub-process once they finish.
The `completionCondition` always takes precedence, so the newly activated elements are silently dropped.
Both operations succeed and the activated elements run as fire-and-forget after the sub-process completes.
The job worker cannot fulfill the completion condition and activate new elements at the same time; these must be separate job completions.

a.text: There is no documented behavior where Zeebe runs the activations first and then completes. The two actions are mutually exclusive in a single job result.

b.text: The docs do not describe silently dropping activations; they state the worker simply cannot combine the two actions in one job completion.

c.text: An ad-hoc sub-process cannot have inner elements running after it completes — the sub-process either cancels remaining instances or waits for them. Fire-and-forget post-completion is not supported.

d.text: Per the docs: the job worker can activate more elements or fulfill the completion condition, but it cannot fulfill both the completion condition and activate new elements at the same time.

explanation: In a job-worker-handled ad-hoc sub-process, the worker receives a job and decides the next step: activate one or more inner elements, or fulfill the completion condition. These are mutually exclusive in a single job completion. There is only one active ad-hoc sub-process job at any time, and the job may be recreated during execution; completing it may also result in a `NOT_FOUND` rejection.
